rulings.Case Title: JAVIER GRAJEDA VS. JOHN GEORGE FORD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2024, Line 1. DEFENDANT JOHN FORD, AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint. Defendants City and County of San Francisco and John Ford's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted. Plaintiff does not dispute that the Code of Civil Procedure allows the cross-complaint, arguing only that the City "waited 10 whole months." (Opp. 3:16-17.) But the City explains it was trying to settle the dispute (Rply. 2:17-26), and the trial date is not until June 9, 2025, so any prejudice will be minor. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDRA ROGERS VS. ESHARES INC. D/B/A CARTA, INC., A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2024, Line 14. CROSS DEFENDANT ALEXANDRA ROGERS' DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Alexandra Rogers' demurrer to Jeffrey Perry's first amended cross-complaint is overruled without prejudice. CCP 430.60 provides: "A demurrer shall distinctly specify the grounds upon which any of the objections to thecross-complaintare taken. Unless it does so, it may be disregarded." Here, Rogers merely "generally demurrers [sic] to all cause of actions [sic]." Also: "Each ground of demurrer must be in a separate paragraph and must state whether it applies to the entirecross-complaint...or to specified causes of action" (CRC 3.1320(a).) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MAJED SHAIR VS. R BAR, INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2024, Line 18. DEFENDANT R BAR, INC., AND LINDSAY PRZYBYL's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants' motion to strike the complaint's punitive damage allegations is denied. Plaintiff pleads he was drinking at defendant bar when the bar's defendant manager objected to his jukebox selection, saying: "We don't play that ghetto music here." Plaintiff noted he had paid for the selection and the manager ordered him to leave. Plaintiff asked for his credit card and a refund for his partially consumed drink. He was then accosted by three men who the manager yelled to "get him." The three slammed plaintiff to the floor, stomped him and jabbed fingers into his eyes, causing "permanent vision damage, facial nerve damage, breathing issues and facial/nose disfigurement." These allegations suffice to support punitive damages at the pleading stage (Civ. Code 3294); the facts can be developed in discovery. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
